name: Bridge / Cut-Kernel Seam -- z3 UNSAT Impossibility Proofs

pyg.reason: not needed -- no graph layer in this proof sim

z3.reason: Core proof engine: each impossibility claim is encoded as a z3 Real-arithmetic formula; UNSAT confirms no counterexample survived the constraint system.

cvc5.reason: not needed -- z3 is the primary proof engine here

sympy.reason: Symbolic derivation of product-state entropy identity (S_AB = S_A + S_B) used as a cross-check on the z3 encoding in Proof 1.

clifford.reason: not needed -- no geometric algebra layer in this lego

geomstats.reason: not needed -- no manifold geometry here

e3nn.reason: not needed -- no equivariant network layer here

rustworkx.reason: not needed -- no dependency graph here

xgi.reason: not needed -- no hypergraph layer here

toponetx.reason: not needed -- no cell complex here

gudhi.reason: not needed -- no persistent homology here

proof1_product_state_no_positive_Ic.claim: A product state (S_AB = S_A + S_B) cannot have I_c = S_B - S_AB > 0
proof1_product_state_no_positive_Ic.interpretation: UNSAT: no assignment of entropy values satisfying product structure and entropy axioms can produce I_c > 0. Proof survived.

proof2_subadditivity_violation_impossibl.claim: I(A:B) = S_A + S_B - S_AB < 0 is impossible under the full entropy axiom system (which includes subadditivity as a foundational axiom)
proof2_subadditivity_violation_impossibl.encoding_note: Subadditivity is encoded as axiom A4. The UNSAT result shows that asserting A4 and its negation simultaneously is contradictory. The sanity SAT check confirms that dropping A4 makes I(A:B)<0 satisfiable, so the UNSAT is not a vacuous artifact.
proof2_subadditivity_violation_impossibl.interpretation: UNSAT main: no assignment consistent with the full entropy axiom system can produce I(A:B) < 0. SAT sanity: without subadditivity the violation is achievable -- confirms the proof is non-trivial. Proof survived.

proof3_pure_state_Ic_equals_SB.claim: For a pure state (S_AB=0), I_c = S_B - S_AB cannot differ from S_B
proof3_pure_state_Ic_equals_SB.interpretation: UNSAT: when S_AB = 0 the expression S_B - S_AB is algebraically identical to S_B. No counterexample survived. Proof confirmed.

bell_state_entropy_assignment_is_sat.note: S_A=1, S_B=1, S_AB=0 is a valid entropy assignment (Bell state).

product_state_entropy_assignment_is_sat.note: S_AB = S_A + S_B with S_A=1, S_B=0.5 is a valid product state.

mixed_entangled_entropy_assignment_is_sa.note: S_A=1, S_B=1, S_AB=0.5 represents a mixed entangled state.

product_state_with_Ic_leq_0_is_sat.note: A product state with I_c <= 0 is satisfiable -- the allowed regime.

subadditivity_holding_is_sat.note: I(A:B) >= 0 is satisfiable -- confirms the proof only rules out the negative side.

pure_state_Ic_equals_SB_is_sat.note: I_c = S_B for pure states is satisfiable -- this is the allowed regime.

max_entropy_boundary_is_sat.note: S_A=1, S_B=1, S_AB=2: maximally mixed product, I_c = -1, consistent.

werner_boundary_entangled_Ic_positive_is.note: Werner-like entangled state: S_A=S_B=1, S_AB in (0,2), I_c>0. This is SAT because the state is NOT product -- consistent with Proof 1 which only rules out product states with I_c>0.

araki_lieb_tight_boundary_is_sat.note: S_AB = S_A - S_B at the Araki-Lieb lower bound is consistent.
